old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-y9na","title":"EDHOC: Missing peer public key validation for low-order points","description":"SECURITY: Ed25519 peer public keys are not validated before use in signature verification.

Locations:
- Line 619: ed25519_verify(peer_pubkey, ...) - no validation of peer_pubkey
- Line 1116: ed25519_verify(peer_pubkey, ...) - no validation of peer_pubkey

While crypto_ed25519_check will likely reject signatures on invalid keys, explicit validation would:
1. Fail faster with clearer error
2. Prevent potential edge cases in the crypto library
3. Follow defense-in-depth principle

Per RFC 8032 Section 5.1.3, public keys should be validated to be valid curve points.

Note: Monocypher's ed25519_check may already handle this, but explicit validation is safer.

Fix: Add validation that peer_pubkey is a valid Ed25519 point before signature verification, or document that Monocypher handles this.

File: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:619,1116","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:45:15.058745Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:45:15.058778Z"}

new_value: Added explicit is_all_zeros(peer_pubkey) check before both ed25519_verify calls (following RFC 8032 invalid key rejection and file's existing pattern) in lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c; verified
